title: Senior SaaS Developer / Technical Lead Needed
description:
Prisma + Supabase + API + Worker/Deployment Debugging



I have an application that is about 80% complete. It was built primarily through Claude, and I’m now at the stage where the remaining issues are beyond what I can confidently troubleshoot on my own.



I am **not** looking for someone to rewrite the app from scratch.



I am looking for a **senior-level developer / technical lead** who is comfortable working in an **AI-assisted development workflow**, where **Claude continues generating most of the code** and you act as the architect, debugger, reviewer, and decision-maker.



I need someone who can:

- review an existing AI-generated codebase

- diagnose bugs and architectural issues

- troubleshoot worker-service, database, Prisma, schema, API, and deployment/container issues

- tell Claude exactly what to do next

- review Claude’s output and refine prompts/instructions as needed

- help me move the application from “mostly built” to stable and production-ready



Important ...

This is **not** a role for a junior developer.

This is also **not** a role for someone who is uncomfortable with AI-generated code.



The ideal person is someone who can look at an existing codebase, quickly understand what’s happening, identify root causes, and use AI tools effectively as a coding assistant rather than fight against them.



You should be strong in most of the following:

- TypeScript / JavaScript

- Prisma

- PostgreSQL / Supabase

- schema design and migrations

- API integrations

- worker/service debugging

- deployment containers

- debugging production/staging issues

- backend architecture

- async flows, queueing, and state issues

- reading and improving AI-generated code



Bonus if you have experience with:

- NestJS

- WebSockets / real-time systems

- rate limiting / retry logic

- observability / logs / metrics

- scaling service-based applications

- Claude Code or similar AI-assisted coding workflows



How we will work is:

- you diagnose and architect

- Claude writes most of the code

- you review and direct Claude

- you step in manually only when necessary to unblock or correct the implementation



In other words:

**Claude is the implementation engine; you are the senior engineer and technical lead.**



In the first phase, I want help with:

- identifying the highest-priority bugs/blockers

- stabilizing the worker/database/deployment issues

- clarifying what Claude should do next

- creating a reliable workflow so I can keep building without getting stuck



Ideal candidate:

- highly practical

- excellent at debugging

- comfortable leading AI-generated development

- able to translate vague technical problems into precise implementation steps

- not thrown off by partially messy or AI-written code

- strong enough to spot when the architecture itself is the issue



If you are a great fit, this can turn into a higher paying, long-term / ongoing role.



Please do **not** apply if you are mainly a prompt hobbyist or a junior developer using AI as a Vibecoder.



I need someone who can truly diagnose systems (without relying on AI), then direct the build intelligently.



-------------------------------------------------



Technical brief linked below. Review it BEFORE applying.





-------------------------------------------------



TO APPLY, please submit answers:



1. What experience do you have debugging Prisma, schema, database, or deployment issues in existing apps?



2. Have you worked in a Claude Code or Abacus / AI-assisted workflow before? If so, how do you use AI effectively without letting it create more problems?



3. How would you approach a codebase that is 90% complete but has worker-service and database bugs?



4. Are you comfortable acting as the senior technical lead while AI handles much of the implementation/writing?



5. What is your English speaking, reading, and writing level, and how many hours a day do you have overlap with the US time zones?

title: SEO ADMIN ASSISTANT
description:
ABOUT SHOP MARKETING PROS:

We are a marketing agency based in the United States with a stateside and international team of 50 professionals. Shop Marketing Pros is a very well-respected and rapidly growing Digital Marketing Company in the Automotive Repair Industry with strong core values and a superb track record of serving its clients better than the competitors. Our key services include Website Design, Digital Advertising, Search Engine Optimization, and Social Media Management. 



Our Core Values are of incredible importance to us. We live by our “SHIELD” so please visit 
/core-values/ and familiarize yourself with our Core Values before continuing. Please continue with this job description and application if you feel you align with these values.



JOB DESCRIPTION:

Shop Marketing Pros is seeking a proactive and highly organized SEO Admin Assistant to support the SEO department in executing and managing monthly client deliverables.



This role plays an important part in keeping our SEO operations organized, on track, and moving forward. You’ll work closely with the SEO Director, SEO Specialists, and Client Success Managers (CSMs) to help ensure deliverables are created, assigned, tracked, and completed accurately and on time.



The ideal candidate is comfortable following structured processes while also stepping back to understand how everything connects. You take pride in your work, stay organized, and are naturally attentive to details. You’re also open to sharing ideas, asking questions, and contributing to improvements that help the team operate more efficiently.



If you enjoy staying organized, keeping things moving, and being part of a collaborative team, you’ll do well in this role.





WHAT SUCCESS LOOKS LIKE IN THIS ROLE:

Monthly SEO deliverables are fully built out, assigned, and consistently moving forward in ClickUp

Nothing falls through the cracks—deadlines are tracked, and delays are addressed early

Client approvals and communications are handled efficiently and accurately

Internal systems (ClickUp, AgencyAnalytics, notes, tracking sheets) are always organized and up to date

SEO Specialists and CSMs have what they need to complete their work without delays

Workflow gaps or inefficiencies are identified and improved proactively

The SEO team feels supported not just with tasks, but with execution and organization





REQUIRED SKILLS:

The ideal SEO Admin Assistant is someone who:

Is extremely organized and detail-oriented

Has a strong sense of ownership and accountability

Is a self-starter who does not wait to be told what to do next

Can follow structured processes while also identifying ways to improve them

Is confident communicating ideas, asking questions, and suggesting better ways to do things

Has strong written communication skills

Can manage multiple tasks and deadlines without losing accuracy

Is comfortable working in project management systems (ClickUp or similar)

Can think both at a detailed level and a big-picture level

Remains calm under pressure and can adjust when priorities shift

Is reliable, consistent, and takes pride in doing things right



Not Required, but helpful:

Experience in administrative support, operations, or project coordination

Experience working in a marketing agency environment

Familiarity with SEO workflows or terminology

Experience with ClickUp, AgencyAnalytics, or similar platforms





JOB RESPONSIBILITIES:

Build, assign, and maintain monthly SEO deliverables within ClickUp

Track all deliverables to ensure deadlines are met, and nothing is missed

Coordinate updates between SEO Specialists, the SEO Director, and CSMs

Send blogs, Google Business Profile posts, and other content for client approval and track revisions

Manage approval workflows to keep projects moving forward efficiently

Ensure all client documentation, notes, and deliverables are accurate and organized

Monitor progress across all SEO work and flag delays or bottlenecks early

Support the SEO team by ensuring clarity, organization, and follow-through across accounts

Act as a liaison between departments to keep communication aligned

Identify workflow inefficiencies and suggest improvements

Provide regular updates to the SEO Director on project status and any issues impacting production



MORE INFO + BENEFITS + PERKS:

This is a full-time, permanent, remote position (8 am to 4 pm Central Time).

13th Month Pay

Unlimited PTO (after 3 months of employment)

8 Paid Holiday Leaves

Paid time off beginning Christmas Eve until New Year’s Day

An extraordinary team/company culture



INSTRUCTIONS FOR APPLICATION:

1. Use this subject line: “SEO Admin Assist - [Your Nickname]."

2. In your cover letter, mention which of our core values resonates with you the most.

3. Include a link to your resume.

4. Use at least 7 Apply Points to qualify for this job.

5. Please DO NOT
 me. All communication must remain within OLJ.

title: Customer Satisfaction & Trustpilot Growth Manager
description:
Customer Satisfaction & Trustpilot Growth Manager





About the role:



We run multiple fashion e-commerce brands operating on a dropshipping model, with Google Shopping as our primary revenue channel. Trustpilot is one of the most critical assets in our business — it directly influences our Google Merchant Center health, our ad account longevity, and whether customers trust us enough to buy again.

Right now, our review scores don't reflect the real quality of our customer experience. Satisfied customers stay silent; frustrated ones leave reviews. We need someone who takes full ownership of fixing that — across all our brands, not just one.

This is not a traditional customer service role. You will sit between the CS team, the
 marketing team, and the founders. You'll be our internal voice of the customer: spotting problems before they become reviews, designing systems that get happy customers to speak up, and proactively pushing ideas that make the experience better.

 

What you'll own:



• Trustpilot score improvement across all brands — this is your primary KPI

• Post-purchase review generation campaigns in Klaviyo — you'll design flows, incentives, and A/B tests together with the
 marketing team

• Customer complaint triage — catching unhappy buyers early (before they hit Trustpilot) and routing them to fast, fair resolutions

• Bridge between CS agents,
 marketing, and the founders — you'll translate customer patterns into actionable improvements

• Proactive improvement proposals — every week you should be identifying one thing we can do better, whether that's a flow timing, a packaging insert, an incentive structure, or a response script

• Review monitoring and response across platforms — Trustpilot, Google Reviews, and any other public-facing review channels

• Tracking and reporting — you'll maintain a live dashboard of all scores, test results, and review trends across all brands

 

What we're looking for:



• Experience in e-commerce customer service, retention, or reputation management

• Comfortable working in Klaviyo or eager to learn fast — you'll be building and editing flows hands-on

• Strong written communication in English (Dutch or French a plus for Benelux brands)

• Data-oriented — you track what you do and can show what moved

• You bring ideas. We don't want someone who waits for a brief. If you see a problem, you come with a proposed fix.

• Able to manage multiple brands simultaneously without dropping balls

 

Nice to have:



• Experience with Trustpilot's platform, review removal requests, or verified review campaigns

• Background in fashion, lifestyle, or beauty e-commerce

• Familiarity with Shopify and post-purchase flows

 

What we offer:



• Fully remote, flexible working hours

• Direct access to the founders — your input shapes real decisions

• Performance bonus tied directly to Trustpilot score improvement and review volume targets

• The chance to build a system from scratch and see the results of your work immediately



How to apply: Qualified candidates may send their application to: coo at abcom dot be

mber to our Brand Manager as you ensure our Amazon listings are optimized and growing.

What does Success Look Like?

What Makes You a Perfect Fit:

Why Join Leap Stores?

How do I apply?



To formally apply for this position, please send a copy of your resume to 
 and use the subject "Your Next Amazon Account Specialist" (don't forget this step).



As an Amazon Account Specialist with Leap Stores, you’ll join a high-performing team focused on execution, accountability, and results. Your expertise will directly impact our brand partners’ growth on the world’s largest e-commerce platform. If you’re ready to take ownership, solve complex challenges, and contribute to a team that’s continuously pushing performance forward, we’d love to connect. Apply today, and let's grow together!

title: Senior WordPress & Webflow Website Developer
description:
Senior WordPress & Webflow Website Developer



Remote Work Requirements (Non-Negotiable)



* Work Hours: 9 AM–6 PM US CST, Monday–Friday

* Reliable Technology: Stable internet connection (?10 Mbps) and dedicated computer

* Hubstaff Usage: Time tracking and productivity monitoring required

* Video Meetings: Camera on for all video calls

* Full-Time Focus: No concurrent employment or freelance contracts



Job Title

Senior WordPress & Webflow Website Developer

Job Description

Buzz Digital Agency is seeking a highly skilled Senior WordPress & Webflow Website Developer to architect, build, and maintain client-facing websites. You’ll leverage both no-code/low-code platforms and custom front-end scripting to deliver polished, performance-optimized sites on time, every time. If you’re passionate about web craftsmanship and thrive in a remote environment, we want to hear from you!

Roles & Responsibilities



* Client Website Builds

* Architect, develop, and deliver fully responsive websites in WordPress (Elementor/Divi/ACF) and Webflow

* Configure themes, templates, custom fields, and page-builder layouts to match design comps

* Domain, Hosting & Deployment

* Manage DNS settings, SSL certificates, hosting environments (staging & production), and backups

* Oversee deployment workflows and rollback procedures

* Custom Front-End Enhancements

* Write clean, maintainable JavaScript, HTML, and CSS for animations, sliders, and form validations

* Implement lightweight scripts to extend CMS functionality without heavy back-end work

* Integrations & Automations

* Connect sites to
-marketing tools (Mailchimp, ActiveCampaign), CRMs (GoHighLevel), payment gateways, and Zapier/Integromat

* Configure and test webhooks and RESTful API calls for seamless data flow

* Performance, Security & SEO

* Optimize images, implement caching and minification for fast load times

* Ensure on-page SEO fundamentals are in place (meta tags, structured data, sitemap)

* Harden sites against common vulnerabilities (XSS, SQL injection, brute-force)

* Collaboration & Version Control

* Partner with designers, marketers, and PMs to translate requirements into scalable solutions

* Maintain code in Git, perform peer reviews, and uphold branching/merging workflows

* Quality Assurance & Maintenance

* Conduct manual (and where feasible, automated) testing to catch regressions

* Troubleshoot, debug, and provide post-launch support—including uptime monitoring and error-log reviews

* Documentation & Client Handoff

* Prepare step-by-step documentation and training materials for client site management

* Provide a 30-day post-launch support window and live handover sessions

* Continuous Improvement

* Research and recommend new tools or processes to boost efficiency and quality

* Share knowledge via internal demos or lunch-and-learn sessions







Nice-to-Have: Mentoring junior developers; experience with Jira/Trello; familiarity with Google Analytics/Tag Manager.

Qualifications



* 5+ years of professional experience in WordPress and Webflow website development

* Advanced skills with Elementor, Divi, ACF, and Webflow CMS

* Strong command of JavaScript, HTML, and CSS for front-end customizations

* Proven track record integrating third-party services via APIs and webhooks

* Deep understanding of DNS, SSL, hosting platforms, and deployment workflows

* Proficient with Git and collaborative development practices

* Excellent English communication—written and verbal

* Highly organized, detail-oriented, and self-motivated in a remote setting





Performance Review & Increase: After a 3-month probationary period and successful milestone delivery, we’ll conduct a formal review with the opportunity to increase base pay toward mid-market rates.



* Benefits & Perks

* Paid Time Off (PTO):

* Earn 40 hours of PTO after completing one full year of employment.

* After three years, your PTO increases to 60 hours annually.

* Unused PTO can roll over, up to a maximum of 80 hours.

* Paid Holidays:

Enjoy paid time off for all major U.S. holidays, including:

* New Year’s Eve & New Year’s Day

* Memorial Day

* Independence Day

* Labor Day

* Thanksgiving Day & the day after

* Christmas Eve & Christmas Day

* Performance Review & Salary Growth:

* Formal review after the 3-month probationary period

* Opportunity for base pay increase based on performance and milestone completion

* Remote Work Culture:

* 100% remote with structured hours and clear expectations

* Collaborative team culture with regular video calls and project alignment



How to Apply

Please submit via OnlineJobs.ph:



1. Your résumé/CV

2. Links to 3 live sites or repositories demonstrating your WordPress, Webflow, and custom JS/CSS work

Turn around edits quickly (24–48 hour turnaround expected)

Responsive communication during working hours

title: Operations Specialist
description:
Adly is a technology, data, and performance-driven portfolio of SaaS subscription and content companies. Our founders have built, acquired, scaled, and successfully exited multiple SaaS, eCommerce, and marketing businesses, many of which are now part of Adly’s growing portfolio. Today, Adly includes social media growth SaaS platforms, AI-driven education technology companies, an
 marketing platform for local businesses, technology-enabled translation services, and more. We continue to expand through new acquisitions and by building innovative products from the ground up.



We are looking for an Operations Specialist to join our team and support the execution of core operational workflows across the business. In this role, you will focus on ensuring efficiency, accuracy, and consistency in day-to-day operations while contributing to process improvement and scalable system development.



Reporting to the Operations Manager, you will take a structured and detail-oriented approach to managing operational tasks, identifying gaps, and improving workflows. This role is ideal for someone who is highly organized, dependable, and comfortable working with data, research, and cross-functional teams in a fast-paced, high-growth environment.



Responsibilities:

Support the execution of daily operational processes with accuracy and attention to detail

Manage and prioritize assigned tasks to ensure consistent and timely delivery

Conduct research and validate information to maintain high standards of data quality

Identify opportunities to enhance processes and support continuous improvement efforts

Develop and document clear, user-friendly standard operating procedures (SOPs)

Collaborate with cross-functional teams to ensure alignment and effective execution

Assist in testing and implementing process improvements, tools, or system updates

Report and follow through on operational issues as needed

Maintain organized and accurate internal records and databases



Skills & Qualifications:

2–3 years of experience in operations or a similar role

Strong organizational skills and a high level of attention to detail

Clear and professional communication skills

Ability to manage multiple responsibilities in a structured and reliable manner

Experience supporting or improving operational processes

Comfortable working with data, research, and administrative tools

Proficiency in G-Suite and Excel/Google Sheets

Familiarity with helpdesk or ticketing systems is an advantage

Demonstrates accountability, consistency, and a collaborative mindset





Preferred:

Experience with subscription-based or SaaS products

Familiarity with payment platforms (Stripe, Recurly, Chargebee, or similar) for processing refunds and managing subscriptions

Experience with project management tools (Asana, Jira, etc.)

Background in QA or product testing — ability to clearly describe bugs and reproduce issues

Experience working in a fast-paced, early-stage environment where things change quickly and ambiguity is normal



Complete this form to apply:

title: Architectural Designer
description:
Job Overview

- Job Title: Architectural Designer (Remote)

- Company: Lynear Studio

- Location: San Jose, California, USA

- Work Setup: Remote

- Work Hours: 2:00 pm - 10:30 pm PST Monday-Friday (5:00 am - 1:30 pm PH Time)



About Lynear Studio

- Lynear Studio is a California-based architecture and design practice located in San Jose, California. Our work is focused on residential projects in the U.S., with an emphasis on clear drawings, efficient coordination, and smooth permit application workflows.

- We are looking for a detail-oriented Architectural Designer to join our remote team. This role will mainly support architectural drafting, permit drawing production, and permit application preparation for California-based projects.

- This is a great opportunity for someone who is strong in Revit, understands how U.S. drawing sets are organized, and wants to grow with a fast-paced studio working on real permit projects.



What You’ll Be Doing



*Core Responsibilities for All Candidates:

- Produce and revise architectural drawings in Revit

- Draft plans, elevations, sections, details, and schedules

- Update permit sets based on redlines and internal comments

- Organize drawing sheets, title blocks, and file structures clearly

- Help prepare permit submission packages and supporting documents

- Maintain accuracy, consistency, and clean Revit models

- Communicate clearly in English and meet deadlines reliably

- Work in U.S. imperial units (feet and inches)



*Mid-Level Candidates Should Also Be Able To:

- Produce a permit or construction drawing set with minimal supervision

- Handle redline revisions efficiently and accurately

- Coordinate with structural, civil, Title 24, or other consultants

- Assist with plan check comment responses and drawing updates

- Understand basic U.S. permit workflow and drawing standards



*Senior-Level Candidates Should Also Be Able To:

- Lead drawing production from design development through permit

- Coordinate directly with consultants and support city comment responses

- Review and QC work prepared by junior tea
mbers

- Help maintain office drawing standards, templates, and workflows

- Work more independently and proactively identify issues before they become problems



What We’re Looking For

Strong Revit proficiency - this is required

- Degree in Architecture or related field preferred

- Strong understanding of architectural drafting and drawing organization

- Experience preparing U.S. permit sets or construction documents

- Strong attention to detail and file organization

- Good written and verbal English communication

- Reliable, responsive, and able to work independently

- U.S. experience is a strong plus

- Experience with AutoCAD, or Bluebeam is a plus

- Familiarity with California residential permit work is a plus



What We Offer

- Competitive Salary

- Paid Time Off (PTO)

- Performance-based compensation growth

- Remote work with a U.S.-based architecture studio

- Opportunity to work on real residential and permit projects in California

- Supportive team environment with clear communication

- Opportunity for long-term growth and increased responsibility



How to Apply

To apply, please
 to 

Use this subject line when applying:

- Architectural Designer - Junior/Mid/Senior Level

In the content, please include:

1.Resume

2.Portfolio in PDF format

- The portfolio should include at least 3-5 relevant work samples

- Please include drafting or permit drawing samples if available, not just renderings

3.A short note answering:

- How many years of Revit experience do you have?

- Have you worked on U.S.-based projects before?

- What type of work are you strongest in: drafting, permit sets, coordination, or project support?

- What is your expected hourly rate?

- Are you comfortable working in U.S. imperial units

- Are you comfortable working in the PST (Pacific Standard Time) time zone

title: IT Engineer
description:
We are a US-based Managed Service Provider (MSP) seeking a skilled and motivated IT Engineer to join our dynamic team. This role offers a unique blend of IT project implementation and day-to-day technical support responsibilities, providing an excellent opportunity to grow your skills in a supportive and collaborative environment.



As an IT Engineer, you will play a key role in maintaining and improving our clients’ IT environments, troubleshooting issues, and contributing to exciting IT projects. If you’re passionate about technology, problem-solving, and delivering exceptional service, we’d love to hear from you!



Key Responsibilities:

**Provide Level 2 technical support for hardware, software, and network issues, escalating complex problems as needed.

*Plan and implement IT projects, ensuring timely and successful delivery.

*Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to desktops, servers, and networks.

*Perform PC maintenance, upgrades, and installations.

*Manage and support user equipment, software, and services remotely.

*Document work thoroughly and update the ticketing system to ensure transparency and efficiency.



Technologies You’ll Work With:

*Operating Systems: Windows and Mac desktop environments, Windows Server.

*Networking: Cisco, Ubiquiti, Fortinet, and general networking concepts.

*Cloud Platforms: Microsoft 365 and Azure.



What We’re Looking For:

**Experienced L2 IT Support role

*Strong troubleshooting skills for hardware, software, and networks.

*Familiarity with Microsoft 365, Windows Server, and networking concepts.

*Excellent communication and customer service skills.

*A proactive, problem-solving mindset with the ability to work independently and as part of a team.



Preferred but Not Required:

*Certifications such as Network+, Microsoft Certified, or CCNA.

*Experience with firewalls, VPNs, and virtual environments.



Why Join Us?



We believe in creating an environment where our tea
mbers can thrive both professionally and personally. Here’s what we offer:



*Career Growth: Paid certifications, training, and structured onboarding to help you advance your skills.

*Attractive Compensation: Competitive salary, performance-based incentives (monthly profit share and end-of-year bonus), and 13th-month pay.

*Health & Wellness: Monthly healthcare allowance to support your well-being.

*Work-Life Balance: 100% remote work with company-provided equipment.

title: Data Entry Specialist
description:

title: AI-Powered Platform Builder — No Advanced Coding Required
description:
Attention: Failure to follow the application instructions will result in automatic disqualification.



ABOUT US



Ninja Customer Care (NCC) is a fast-growing AI-assisted customer experience company serving nutraceutical and telemedicine brands in the US market. We run a team of agents powered by real-time AI guidance and are building a Customer Intelligence Platform that will serve brands, networks, and DTC companies at scale.



We are a startup with serious traction, a clear product roadmap, and a founder who knows exactly what needs to be built. We are not figuring out the vision — we need someone who can execute it using today's AI tools.



THIS IS NOT A TRADITIONAL DEVELOPER ROLE



We are not looking for someone who writes complex code from scratch all day. The world has changed. AI coding tools like Claude Code, Cursor, and GitHub Copilot now do the heavy lifting of code generation.



What we need is someone who knows how to:

• Use AI coding tools to build real, working software

• Connect different systems and tools together via APIs

• Read and review AI-generated code to make sure it is correct

• Deploy working software to a server

• Communicate clearly about what is being built and why



If you have been using AI coding tools to build projects and you understand how systems connect — this role was written for you.



WHAT YOU WILL BE BUILDING



We are building a platform on top of our existing tools — Konnektive, CheckoutChamp, Dialpad, 
, and ActiveCampaign. You are not replacing these tools. You are connecting them and building an intelligence layer on top.



The platform includes:



• A data layer that pulls information from all our tools into one place

• A dashboard that shows clients their real-time performance

• Automated workflows that trigger alerts based on business rules

• A client portal where clients can log in and see their own data

• A churn prediction system that flags at-risk customers before they cancel

• A contact deflection system that handles routine customer inquiries automatically



You will receive a clear written spec for every module before you build it. You will use AI coding tools to build it. The founder will be involved throughout — you are never building blind.





WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR



MUST HAVE



• Real experience using AI coding tools to build something

  — Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot, or similar

  — You must show us something you have actually built using these tools

  — This is the most important requirement. Everything else is secondary.



• Basic understanding of APIs

  — You know what an API is, you have connected tools together before,

    you can read API documentation and figure out how to use it



• Ability to review and understand code

  — You do not need to write every line from scratch

  — But you need to read AI-generated code and know if it looks right



• Ability to deploy to a server

  — You have put something live before, even something small

  — DigitalOcean, AWS, Heroku — any of these is fine



• Strong English communication

  — You will talk to the founder daily

  — Clear, direct, no confusion



• Organized and reliable

  — You hit deadlines, you flag problems early, you do not disappear



NICE TO HAVE



• Familiarity with any of our tools: Konnektive, CheckoutChamp,

  Dialpad, 
, ActiveCampaign



• Experience building dashboards or data visualization



• Any exposure to databases — PostgreSQL, MySQL, or similar



• Background working with US-based clients or companies



YOU DO NOT NEED



• A computer science degree

• Years of traditional software engineering experience

• To write complex algorithms or advanced code from scratch

• A big portfolio — one or two real AI-built projects is enough



HOW THIS WORKS



We start with a small paid trial project — one module, clear scope, fixed price ($300–$500). This is real work that goes into production if you deliver it correctly.



If you deliver — we move to a full-time monthly retainer. Long-term this role grows as the platform grows. We take care of people who perform.



You will never be left guessing what to build. Every sprint starts with a written spec. The founder is available daily. We move fast and we communicate clearly.



HOW TO APPLY



Send the following to 
 :



1. A brief introduction — who you are, where you are based, what you have been building lately (3–5 sentences is fine)



2. One example of something you built using an AI coding tool

   — A link, a short video, a GitHub repo, a screenshot with explanation

   — It does not have to be big or impressive — it just has to be real



3. Answer this question in 4–6 sentences:

   "If I asked you to connect two different software tools so that data from one automatically appears in the other, how would you approach that?"



Applications without all three items will not be reviewed.

We read every application that follows the instructions.

title: Cold Email & LinkedIn Outreach Specialist (Smartlead, Heyreach, Zapmail)
description:
Algominds is a London-based AI sales company hiring a Cold
 & LinkedIn Outreach Specialist based in the Philippines to manage and optimize outreach campaigns across our client accounts.



This is a hands-on execution role. You will be building, managing, and optimizing cold
 and LinkedIn outreach campaigns daily using specific tools. This is not a general VA role.



YOU MUST HAVE DIRECT EXPERIENCE WITH THESE THREE PLATFORMS:



1. Smartlead.ai — campaign setup, mailbox rotation, lead management, warmup settings, master inbox management

2. 
 — LinkedIn automation, multi-sender campaigns, connection requests, messaging sequences

3. Zapmail.ai — domain setup, mailbox provisioning, DNS configuration, deliverability management



If you have not used all three of these tools, please do not apply. We will verify your experience on our onboarding call.



WHAT THE ROLE INVOLVES:



Build and launch cold
 campaigns in Smartlead across multiple client workspaces. Set up and manage LinkedIn outreach sequences in Heyreach. Provision and maintain
 infrastructure including domains, mailboxes, and DNS records in Zapmail. Monitor deliverability metrics, bounce rates, and warmup health daily. Manage lead uploads, list segmentation, and campaign targeting. Respond to and categorize replies in Smartlead master inbox. A/B test subject lines, copy, and sending schedules to improve open and reply rates. Report weekly on campaign performance across all client accounts. Coordinate with our London-based team via Slack.



COMPENSATION:



$300 per month base salary paid via Wise on the 1st of every month. Performance bonuses based on campaign metrics including reply rates and meetings booked. Top performers managing multiple client accounts earn $700 to $1,000+ per month. No cap on bonuses.



REQUIREMENTS:



Based in the Philippines. Proven hands-on experience with Smartlead.ai, 
, and Zapmail.ai. This is non-negotiable. Understanding of
 deliverability including SPF, DKIM, DMARC, domain reputation, and inbox placement. Experience writing or editing cold
 copy and LinkedIn messaging sequences. Comfortable managing multiple client campaigns simultaneously. Excellent written English. Reliable internet connection and laptop or PC. Wise account or willingness to create one. Available during overlapping hours with UK timezone for weekly check-ins.



PREFERRED BUT NOT REQUIRED:



Experience with Clay, Instantly, Lemlist, or similar outreach tools. Familiarity with LinkedIn Sales Navigator. Experience working in an agency environment managing multiple client accounts. Understanding of B2B lead generation and ICP targeting.



IN YOUR APPLICATION PLEASE INCLUDE:



1. Which of the three tools Smartlead, Heyreach, and Zapmail you have the most experience with and for how long

2. A brief example of campaign results you have achieved such as open rates, reply rates, or meetings booked

3. How many client accounts or campaigns you have managed at once



Generic applications that do not address these three points will not be reviewed.

title: 2D Motion Graphics Designer - Social Media & YouTube Content (After Effects + Premiere Pro)
description:
We are a growing social media content agency producing weekly Reels, TikToks, animated posts, and YouTube videos for clients across various industries. We are actively expanding our client base and building a reliable creative team.

We are looking for a talented 2D Motion Graphics Designer to join us long-term as a dedicated freelancer. This is NOT a one-time project — we need someone who can grow with us from part-time to full-time as we sign more clients.

What you will do:



Create animated Reels and short-form videos (30 seconds to 1.5 minutes) with custom 2D motion graphics

Create animated posts with motion elements (carousels, stories, static with animation)

Produce motion graphics for YouTube videos (intros, lower thirds, transitions, animated sequences) for clients who need longer-form content

Design kinetic typography, smooth transitions, animated text, and scroll-stopping hooks

Create custom graphic elements, icons, and visual assets for each client

Follow brand guidelines and maintain visual consistency across all deliverables

Deliver platform-ready files optimized for Instagram, TikTok,
, and YouTube (9:16, 1:1, 4:5, 16:9)

Work volume will increase as we onboard new clients — we want someone ready to scale with us



Required skills (non-negotiable):



Adobe After Effects — this is our primary tool, you MUST be highly proficient

Adobe Premiere Pro — for video editing, assembly, and final export

Adobe Illustrator and/or Photoshop — for creating graphic elements and visual assets

Minimum 2 years of hands-on experience in 2D motion graphics

Strong understanding of social media content — you know what makes people stop scrolling

Clean animation principles: proper easing, timing, rhythm, and visual hierarchy

Ability to deliver within 24-48 hour turnaround per piece

Reliable internet connection and responsive communication



Nice to have:



Basic Cinema 4D or Blender knowledge for simple 3D elements

Experience with YouTube content (intros, outros, animated overlays, chapter transitions)

Experience producing content for brands or agencies

Understanding of current social media trends and formats

Ability to manage multiple client projects at the same time



DO NOT APPLY if:



You only use CapCut, Canva, or template-based tools — we need real After Effects work

You have less than 2 years of motion graphics experience

You cannot provide a showreel or portfolio with real project examples

You are looking for a one-time gig — this is a long-term position



HOW TO APPLY — PLEASE READ CAREFULLY:

Your application MUST include all 4 of the following. Incomplete applications will not be reviewed:



A link to your showreel or portfolio (Vimeo, Google Drive, Behance, or personal website) showing your best 2D motion graphics work

At least 3 examples of content you have created (Reels, animated posts, YouTube motion graphics, or short-form videos)

Your hourly rate in USD

A short message (2-3 sentences maximum) telling us what makes you stand out



To increase your chances of being selected, shortlisted candidates will be asked to complete a brief test based on a real client brief. This is your opportunity to show us your skills, speed, and creative thinking in a real working scenario.

We pay weekly. No delays, no excuses — you deliver quality work, you get paid on time, every week.

We are building something long-term and we want the right person by our side. If you are consistent, reliable, and passionate about motion design, we want to hear from you.

title: Video Editor (Remote)
description:
Video Editor (Remote) | U.S. Family Law Brand



Are you creative, detail oriented, and serious about storytelling that actually moves people to act? We are hiring a Full Time Video Editor to turn raw attorney/education footage into polished, on brand videos that inform, inspire, and convert. This is a production role with clear output targets. If you need hand holding, this isn’t for you.



Job Description:

You will own the end to end video pipeline, from assembling rough cuts to publishing on YouTube, Instagram, TikTok, and our website. You will deliver clean edits, strong pacing, crisp captions, and optimized thumbnails, all while following brand and legal compliance guidelines for a family law practice. Your work will be measured, not guessed: output, quality, and audience growth.



Key Responsibilities:

• Produce attorney/educational videos for YouTube and short form for Reels/TikTok/Shorts.

• Add captions, lower thirds (attorney name/title), thumbnails, and CTA end cards.

• Export in multiple aspect ratios: 16:9, 9:16, 1:1.

• Upload and optimize across platforms (metadata, chapters, tags, end screens, cards).

• Apply YouTube SEO best practices (titles, descriptions, chapters, tags, schema).

• Maintain structured project folders in Google Drive (or other tools) with naming conventions and backups.

• Manage versions and handoffs so anyone can find what they need in under a minute.

• Track performance (watch time, retention, CTR, follower growth) and propose improvements.

• Coordinate schedules with attorneys and freelance videographers; hit deadlines without delays.

• Ensure legal disclaimers and confidentiality standards are applied consistently.



Performance Expectations:

1. Output

 • 4–6 long form attorney/educational videos per month.

 • 30–40 short form reels/TikToks per month.

 • 5–10 static assets (thumbnails, carousels) per month.



2. Quality & Branding

 • 100% of videos include captions, lower thirds, CTA end cards, and required aspect ratios.

 • 95% brand consistency (logo, fonts, colors, tone).

 • 100% required legal disclaimers included; captions properly timed and readable.



3. Engagement & Reach (within 6–12 months)

 • +15–20% average video watch time

 • +10–15% social engagement (likes, comments, shares)

 • +5–8% YouTube CTR via improved thumbnails/titles

 • +20% growth in YouTube subscribers or TikTok/Instagram followers



4. Collaboration & Efficiency

 •  95% of content delivered on schedule per content calendar.

 • 80–90% of attorney filming sessions successfully coordinated with videographers.

 • 100% of final edits reviewed and approved within 72 hrs of draft delivery.



Qualifications:

 • Strong editing skills in Adobe Premiere Pro or DaVinci Resolve (After Effects or Motion a plus).

 • At least 2+ years of experience editing for YouTube and/or social media platforms.

 • Sharp sense of pacing, structure, and audience retention (hooks, pattern breaks, CTA placement).

 • Excellent organization: you respect file structures, version control, and deadlines.

 • Professionalism and discretion with sensitive family law topics.

 • Proficiency with Google Workspace and project tools (Asana, Trello, or similar).

 • Bonus: YouTube SEO, GA4/analytics, or content marketing experience.



Work Schedule:

 • Full time (40 hours per week).

 • Must be available to work during U.S. Eastern Standard Time (EST) business hours for team coordination.



Screening Questions (answer all to be considered)

1. Do you have at least 2 years of video editing experience? Briefly describe the project types and your role.

2. Have you created content for YouTube, Instagram Reels, TikTok, or Shorts? Share links and note what you owned (edit, captions, thumbnail, etc.).

3. What editing software do you primarily use, and what’s your level of expertise?

4. How do you manage file organization and version control across multiple concurrent projects?

5. Link your reel/portfolio that best demonstrates storytelling, pacing, and results (watch time, CTR, or growth if available).



----------------------------------------------

How to Apply

 • Submit your resume, cover letter, and answers to all screening questions directly.

 • Use the subject line: “Video Editor – [Your Name] – Empower.”

 • Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.



If you can deliver consistent, on brand edits, move fast without sacrificing accuracy, and back your creative choices with data, we want to see your work!

title: Junior Integration Specialist (Client Services)
description:
We are seeking a motivated Junior Integration Specialist to join our Managed Service Provider (MSP) team. In this role, you won't just manage one system, you will be the "bridge builder" for our diverse portfolio of business clients, helping to connect their various software platforms so their data flows seamlessly.

 

This is a client-facing technical role designed for someone who understands system logic and is eager to master enterprise-level tools like Boomi, Salesforce, and Power Automate across multiple different client environments. You will work under the guidance of senior specialists to build, monitor, and troubleshoot the automated "pipes" that keep our clients' businesses running efficiently.

 

Key Responsibilities

      • Client Integration Monitoring & Support

        o Monitor daily integration logs across multiple client environments to ensure data is syncing correctly.

        o Identify, troubleshoot, and resolve integration errors or data sync failures for various client systems.

        o Maintain clear technical documentation for each client’s unique integration workflows and data mappings.



      • Workflow Automation for Clients

         o Help build and test automated workflows to reduce manual data entry for our clients' business teams.

         o Assist in setting up automated alerts, notifications, and scheduled tasks within client environments.

         o Update and optimize existing client automations as their business requirements evolve.



      • Data Integrity & CRM Support

        o Assist with data cleanup, mapping, and bulk imports into client CRMs and other business tools.

        o Help configure client system settings (custom fields, user permissions, etc.) to support new integrations.

        o Verify that client data remains consistent across their various platforms (e.g., ERP to CRM sync).



      • Requirements Gathering & Consulting

        o Partner with client stakeholders to understand their business processes and document their technical requirements.

        o Participate in the testing and QA phase of new system rollouts for clients.

        o Actively train on our core tech stack (Boomi, Salesforce, Power BI/Automate) to take on more complex client builds.

 

Qualifications

Required

     • Technical Aptitude: A strong interest in system logic and a desire to build a career in technical integrations and automation.

     • Basic API Knowledge: A foundational understanding of what an API is and familiarity with data formats like JSON.

     • Comfort with APIs: Ability to read technical documentation to understand how different software platforms can be connected.

     • Business Process Mindset: Ability to look at a client's manual task and visualize how it could be automated.

     • Customer Service Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication; ability to explain technical status to non-technical client contacts.



Preferred (Nice to Have)

     • 0–2 years of experience in an MSP, IT support, or junior technical role.

     • Exposure to any CRM (Salesforce, HubSpot, etc.) or automation tool (Zapier, Power Automate, etc.).

     • Experience using Excel for data organization or basic troubleshooting.



Why Join Us?



We believe in creating an environment where our tea
mbers can thrive both professionally and personally. Here’s what we offer:



*Career Growth: Paid certifications, training, and structured onboarding to help you advance your skills.

*Attractive Compensation: Competitive salary, performance-based incentives (monthly profit share and end-of-year bonus), and 13th-month pay.

*Health & Wellness: Monthly healthcare allowance to support your well-being.

*Work-Life Balance: 100% remote work with company-provided equipment.

*Supportive Culture: Be part of a forward-thinking team that values collaboration, innovation, and having fun while doing great work.



What Sets Us Apart?



*Performance-Based Rewards: Monthly profit sharing and bonuses for high-performing tea
mbers.

*Continuous Learning: We invest in your growth with paid certifications and training.

*Collaborative Environment: Work with a team that values your input and supports your success.

*Flexibility: Enjoy the freedom of remote work while contributing to impactful projects.



How to Apply

If you’re ready to take your IT career to the next level, we’d love to hear from you! Apply now and join a team that’s passionate about technology, growth, and delivering exceptional service.



**Submit your application and include the numbers (2586) in your subject line. This helps us ensure you’ve read the full job description.



**Please also attach your updated CV to your application or provide a link to your CV. Applications without a CV will not be considered.

*** The working hours for this role are 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM CST with an hour of Lunch break, Monday to Friday.



We look forward to hearing from you.

title: Private Equity: Manager of Acquisitions & Asset Management
description:
About Us

Three Little Pigs Investment Services is a multifamily real estate private equity firm with 450+ units under management. We acquire, reposition, and manage value-add apartment communities through a vertically integrated platform spanning acquisitions, property management, renovations, and investor syndication. We're growing fast and building the team to match.



About the Role

This is a dual-function role covering both sides of the investment lifecycle. On the acquisitions side, you'll source deals, build underwriting models, manage the pipeline, and coordinate due diligence through closing. On the asset management side, you'll drive portfolio performance, oversee property operations, produce investor reporting, and manage lender compliance.

You'll report directly to the Managing Principal and have high visibility across every phase of the investment cycle. This is a high-autonomy, high-ownership role for someone who wants to build something, not just maintain it.



What You'll Do



Acquisitions:

Source on-market and off-market multifamily deals through broker relationships and direct outreach

Underwrite deals using dynamic Excel models (IRR, equity multiple, cash-on-cash, cap rate, DSCR)

Review OMs, rent rolls, T12 financials, and operating statements

Manage the deal tracker, prepare investment memos, and draft LOIs

Coordinate due diligence from LOI through closing



Asset Management:

Oversee weekly property operations with the PM team; monitor occupancy, collections, and maintenance

Manage monthly financial reporting, variance analysis, and operating budgets

Prepare quarterly investor update letters and coordinate K-1 delivery

Manage lender compliance reporting and loan covenant tracking

Run annual sell/refi/hold analysis on each asset



What We're Looking For

Required:



3+ years in commercial real estate acquisitions, asset management, or investment analysis

Strong Excel financial modeling skills (DCF, sensitivity analysis)

Familiarity with multifamily operating metrics and deal structures

Experience with investor reporting and property management oversight

Excellent written communication skills; high attention to detail

Self-directed and comfortable in a lean, fast-moving environment



Preferred:

CPA License

Experience with AppFolio, QuickBooks Online, Notion, or Property Meld

Familiarity with AI tools for underwriting and workflow automation

Knowledge of syndication structures and LP/GP relationships



Position Details

Location: Remote (US hours required)

Type: Full-Time

Reports To: Managing Principal

title: Google Ads Manager for Call-Driven Local Clinic
description:
Google Ads Manager for Call-Driven Local Clinic



We are looking for a Google Ads Manager to take over and improve a local clinic account where almost all conversions come through phone calls, not forms.



This is not a basic traffic-management role. We need someone who understands how to improve lead quality, reduce wasted spend, and diagnose whether issues are coming from campaign structure, search terms, conversion tracking, messaging, landing pages, or call handling.



We want someone who can think like an operator, not just make surface-level recommendations.



What you’ll be responsible for



Managing and optimizing Google Ads Search campaigns for a local service business

Auditing campaign structure, keyword strategy, match types, negative keywords, bidding, ad copy, assets, geotargeting, devices, and dayparting

Reviewing call-heavy conversion setups and identifying tracking gaps

Improving lead quality, not just increasing click volume

Aligning campaigns and landing pages with service intent

Providing clear reporting on what is proven by data versus what still needs better tracking



What we’re looking for



Strong experience with Google Ads Search for local lead generation

Experience with call-driven accounts where phone calls are the main conversion

Strong understanding of search term analysis, negative keyword strategy, device and geographic performance, and conversion-focused optimization

Ability to identify when tracking is incomplete and explain how that affects recommendations

Ability to think beyond the ad account and diagnose real business bottlenecks

Clear, direct communication with no fluff



Preferred



Experience with healthcare, clinics, urgent care, or other high-intent local service accounts

Experience with call tracking platforms and offline conversion strategy

Experience improving lead quality in accounts where raw conversion counts can be misleading



To apply, please answer the following:



How would you audit a local Google Ads account where about 99% of conversions are phone calls?

What can Google Ads tell you about phone call conversions, and what can it not tell you without additional tracking?

If tracking is incomplete, how would that affect your audit conclusions and recommendations?

What are the first 5 to 10 things you would review in a call-driven local account?

How would you determine whether poor performance is caused by campaign structure, search terms, conversion setup, landing pages, or front-desk handling?



Please keep your answers practical and specific. We are not looking for generic Google Ads best practices. We are looking for someone who can diagnose, prioritize, and improve a real call-based account.

title: Marketing Assistant
description:
Position: Marketing Assistant

Department: Marketing

Reports To: Owner / CEO

Job Type: Part-time — Contractor

Hours: 40–60 hours per month (with room to grow)

Compensation: 500–650 PHP per hour, based on experience



About the Role

I’m looking for a detail-oriented, creative, and execution-focused Marketing Assistant to support the day-to-day content and marketing operations at Valley Virtual Operations. This is NOT a strategy role — the strategy and direction are already in place. What I need is someone who can take the plan and run with it: creating content, scheduling posts, building out ad creative, and keeping my content library organized.



You won’t be making high-level decisions or managing campaigns. You will be the hands that bring the vision to life — consistently, accurately, and on time. This is a part-time role right now, and that’s intentional. But I’m in it for the long haul and I’m looking for someone who is too. As VVO continues to grow, so will the content, the marketing, and this role — with a real possibility of transitioning to full-time for the right person.



About the Company

Valley Virtual Operations (VVO) is a systems and operations business serving female online entrepreneurs who are ready to scale without the chaos. I specialize in process buildouts, systems strategy, and digital products that help online business owners — especially coaches and agencies — scale beyond $250k and toward $500k+ without burning out or staying stuck in the weeds of their business.



I value efficiency, clarity, and communication. My environment is collaborative, supportive, and built on doing great work while still being human. If you love helping things “just work,” you’ll fit right in.



Responsibilities Will Include

Content Creation

Creating Instagram Reels and short-form video content (hooks, b-roll concepts, and edits)

Designing carousel posts, static graphics, and story content primarily in Canva

Formatting captions and uploading graphics across platforms

Scheduling content using my content management tools

 Ads Support

Loading ad campaigns into Meta/Facebook Ads Manager (copy and paste — no campaign strategy required)

Creating ad creative and basic mockups using provided brand assets

Training will be provided for my ad upload process — you just need to be comfortable inside the platform

Design & Visual Support

Building out mockups, slide decks, and other design elements as needed

Keeping brand consistency across all visual content

Organization & Engagement

Managing and organizing my content library so assets are easy to find and use

Handling basic engagement (responding to comments, DMs as directed) across platforms

Supporting with general marketing tasks as they come up

Admin Support

General admin tasks that keep the business running day-to-day — not always marketing-related, but always important

Light inbox support and drafting communications as directed

Organizing files, folders, and digital assets so everything is easy to find

Supporting with miscellaneous tasks and one-off projects as they come up



Tools & Platforms I Use

Canva, ClickUp, Meta Business Suite, Metricool, CapCut, Instagram,
, TikTok, GetPoppy.Ai, Claude.



This Position IS for You If…

You’re a strong Canva user who can create polished, on-brand content quickly and confidently

You have at least 3–5 years of experience in a marketing or social media execution role

You’re comfortable inside Meta/Facebook Ads Manager (or willing to learn my process fast)

You can create Reels and short-form video content that actually looks good

You’re organized, reliable, and thrive on a clear task list — you don’t need someone to manage you

You understand branding and can maintain visual consistency across content types

You’re someone who communicates proactively and hits deadlines without being chased

You’re looking for a long-term position — this is part-time now, but I’m building toward something bigger

BONUS: You’re familiar with Maria Wendt’s content style and aesthetic



This Position is NOT for You If…

Missing deadlines is not something you take seriously — this role requires consistent, on-time delivery and communication.

You struggle with attention to detail or consistent follow-through on tasks

You’re not open to feedback or direction on creative work

You’re looking for a quick gig — I’m looking for someone invested in building something long-term



Compensation & Details

This is a part-time contractor role at 40–60 hours per month, with room to grow as the business and content needs expand. I’m looking for a long-term fit — someone who wants to grow with me, not just fill a slot.



One last thing — if you've read this far, you're already ahead of the crowd. To confirm you've reviewed the full job description, include the keyword "SYSTEMS" in your application where prompted. Applications that do not include this word will not be reviewed.



The Hiring Process

I’ll be upfront: I’m not just looking to fill a seat. I’m looking for someone exceptional — a long-term partner in the business who takes their work seriously and shows up with excellence. Because of that, this is going to be an intentional, in-depth hiring process to make sure we’re the right fit for each other.



Step 1: Application — Submit your application using the link below

Step 2: Interview — A conversation to get to know you and your experience

Step 3: Paid Test Project — A short, compensated project to see your skills in action

Step 4: Final Offer — The right fit gets the role



How to Apply

Applications are being accepted on a rolling basis and will close on April 10th — or sooner if the right candidates come through early. Don’t wait. I’m aiming to have someone onboarded by April 20th.



Please do not DM or
. Applications submitted outside of the form will not be considered.






I look forward to reviewing your application. Thank you for taking the time to fill it out.

title: 3D Motion Graphics Designer - Social Media & YouTube Content (Cinema 4D / Blender + After Effects)
description:
We are a growing social media content agency producing weekly Reels, TikToks, animated posts, and YouTube videos for clients across various industries. We are actively expanding our client base and building a reliable creative team.

 

We are looking for a skilled 3D Motion Graphics Designer to join us long-term as a dedicated freelancer. This is NOT a one-time project — we need someone who can grow with us from part-time to full-time as we sign more clients.

 

 

WHAT YOU WILL DO:

 

- Create 3D animated Reels and short-form videos (30 seconds to 1.5 minutes) with high-quality 3D motion graphics for Instagram, TikTok, and

- Produce 3D motion graphics for longer YouTube videos (5 to 30+ minutes) including 3D intros, 3D transitions, animated sequences, and 3D visual storytelling elements

- Produce 3D product renders, 3D text, 3D scenes, and 3D animated elements for social media content

- Create animated posts with 3D motion elements (carousels, stories, static with 3D animation)

- Combine 3D elements with 2D compositing in After Effects for polished final deliverables

- Model, light, texture, and animate 3D objects and scenes

- Follow brand guidelines and maintain visual consistency across all deliverables

- Deliver platform-ready files optimized for Instagram, TikTok,
, and YouTube (9:16, 1:1, 4:5, 16:9)

- Work volume will increase as we onboard new clients — we want someone ready to scale with us

 

 

REQUIRED SKILLS (non-negotiable):

 

- Cinema 4D and/or Blender — you MUST be highly proficient in at least one 3D software

- Adobe After Effects — for compositing, 2D motion elements, and final polish

- Adobe Premiere Pro — for video editing, assembly, and final export

- Strong 3D skills: modeling, lighting, texturing, rendering, and animation

- Minimum 2 years of hands-on experience in 3D motion graphics

- Strong understanding of social media content — you know what grabs attention

- Clean animation principles: proper easing, timing, camera movements, and visual impact

- Ability to deliver within 48-72 hour turnaround per piece

- Reliable internet connection and responsive communication

 

 

NICE TO HAVE:

 

- Octane Render, Redshift, or other GPU renderers for faster, higher-quality output

- Experience with 3D product visualization and product animation

- Experience with YouTube content (3D intros, outros, animated overlays)

- Experience producing content for brands or agencies

- Understanding of current social media trends and formats

- Ability to manage multiple client projects at the same time

 

 

DO NOT APPLY IF:

 

- You do not know Cinema 4D or Blender — this is a 3D-focused role

- You only do 3D modeling without animation — we need motion, not still renders

- You have less than 2 years of 3D motion graphics experience

- You cannot provide a showreel or portfolio with real 3D project examples

- You are looking for a one-time gig — this is a long-term position

 

 

HOW TO APPLY — PLEASE READ CAREFULLY:

 

Your application MUST include all 4 of the following. Incomplete applications will not be reviewed:

 

1. A link to your showreel or portfolio (Vimeo, Google Drive, Behance, or personal website) showing your best 3D motion graphics work — we want to see modeling, animation, lighting, and rendering quality

2. At least 3 examples of 3D content you have created (3D Reels, 3D product animations, 3D social media content, or 3D YouTube intros)

3. Your hourly rate in USD

4. A short message (2-3 sentences maximum) telling us what 3D software you use and what makes you stand out

 

To increase your chances of being selected, shortlisted candidates will be asked to complete a brief test based on a real client brief. This is your opportunity to show us your 3D skills, speed, and creative thinking in a real working scenario.

 

We pay weekly. No delays, no excuses — you deliver quality work, you get paid on time, every week.

 

We are building something long-term and we want the right person by our side. If you are consistent, reliable, and passionate about 3D motion design, we want to hear from you.

title: Sales & Engagement VA for Online Education Business (Setter + Social Media + Tracking)
description:
About Us

We run a 12-week online program that helps educators and education professionals build profitable teaching businesses without quitting their jobs. We work with passionate, mission-driven educators who are tired of salary caps and institutional red tape. They want to own their expertise and build income on their terms. We're growing fast and need a sharp, sales-minded VA to help us scale.



What You'll Do

Your primary job is to move conversations toward enrollment.

You'll be the first voice prospects hear from us. You'll engage with cold and warm leads on social media, respond to DMs, qualify interest, book discovery calls for our co-founder, and track everything so nothing falls through the cracks.

This role requires flexibility and eagerness to learn. You'll need to step in and be trained on whatever the business needs as we grow — whether that's refining DM scripts, testing new outreach strategies, or supporting other areas of the business. We're looking for someone who sees this as an opportunity to grow with us, not just complete tasks.

Daily Responsibilities:

DM Management & Lead Engagement



Monitor and respond to messages across LinkedIn, Instagram,
, and TikTok

Use our proven scripts to move cold and warm leads toward discovery calls

Follow up consistently with prospects using our outreach sequences

Identify high-intent prospects and book calls with our closer



Lead Qualification



Ask qualifying questions before booking calls (current role, pain points, timeline)

Flag hot leads to prioritize

Politely redirect poor-fit prospects



Call Booking & Coordination



Send Calendly links to qualified leads

Confirm calls 24 hours in advance and send reminders

Update tracker with call outcomes (showed / no-showed / rescheduled / closed)



Tracking & Reporting



Update DM tracker daily (who was contacted, responses, next steps)

Update sales tracker after each discovery call

Provide weekly reports on activity and results



Light Social Media Engagement (As needed)



Like and comment on posts from warm leads

Respond to story replies and engagement

Maintain a warm, relational presence online





Who You Are

Must-Haves:



? Sales experience — You've done appointment-setting, lead generation, or sales support before. You understand how to move conversations toward a goal.

? Amazing conversational English — You write like a real person, not a robot. Your grammar is flawless and your tone is warm and natural.

? Admin skills — You're organized, detail-oriented, and can manage multiple conversations and tracking systems without dropping the ball.

? Eager learner — You're excited to be trained on new systems, tools, and strategies. You don't just want to complete tasks — you want to understand the business and grow with it.

? Cultural fit — You're proactive, positive, and collaborative. You bring solutions, not just problems. You communicate clearly and take ownership of your work.



Strong Pluses:



???? Previous experience in education, coaching, or course-creator industries

???? Experience with CRM systems or pipeline management tools

???? Comfortable working with U.S.-based clients in similar time zones



Personality Fit:



Relational and warm — You genuinely enjoy connecting with people. Our audience is educators — they can spot inauthenticity immediately.

Results-driven — You're energized by hitting goals and seeing conversations turn into booked calls and closed sales.

Comfortable with sales — You don't shy away from asking for the call. You see it as helping, not pushing.

Fast-moving and adaptable — You're comfortable with change and evolving systems. You don't need everything to be perfect before you act.

Proactive and enthusiastic — You see what needs to happen and make it happen without being told.

Coachable — You take feedback well and improve quickly.





Compensation & Hours

Starting Rate: $4.00/hour

Hours: 20–25 hours per week to start (potential to grow to 30–40 hours based on performance and business needs)

Commission: $125 per closed sale (begins after your first month of employment)

Payment: Weekly via PayPal, Wise, or your preferred method

How Earnings Work:

Month 1 (Training Period):



You'll earn your base hourly rate while you learn our systems, scripts, and processes

No commission during Month 1 — this is your ramp-up period



Month 2 and Beyond:



Base pay continues at $4/hour

You earn $125 commission for every prospect you engage with who enrolls in our program

Commission is paid weekly along with your base pay



Commission Eligibility: You earn commission when a prospect you directly engaged with (via DM, follow-up, or booking coordination) enrolls in our program. We track this through our DM and sales trackers, so you'll always know which leads are yours.

Example Monthly Earnings (After Month 1):



Base (25 hours/week × 4 weeks × $4/hour): $400/month

Commission (3 closed sales/month × $125): +$375/month

Total potential: $775+/month



High Performer Example:



Base: $400/month

Commission (5 closed sales/month × $125): +$625/month

Total: $1,025+/month



As you prove yourself and take on more responsibility, we're open to raising your base rate and increasing commission structure.



Work Schedule & Availability

Required Working Hours:

We need someone available during U.S. Central Time (CST) business hours, specifically:



Monday–Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM CST (at least partial overlap required)

Some flexibility required for evening coverage (7:00–9:00 PM CST) 2–3 nights per week when discovery calls happen



You don't need to work all those hours, but you need to be responsive during those windows.

Start Date: As soon as possible (we're ready to onboard the right person immediately)



How to Apply

IMPORTANT: Applications missing ANY of these requirements will be deleted without review.

To apply, you MUST:

1. Use exactly 9 Application Points (AP) when submitting your application through OnlineJobs.ph

2. Use this exact
 subject line:

"Sales VA – [Your First Name] – 9 AP"

3. Submit a short video no more than 2-minutes on Loom, unlisted YouTube, or another platform where you:



Introduce yourself

Explain why you're interested in a sales/setter role (not just general VA work)

Confirm your internet speed and availability during CST hours



4. Include in your application message on OnlineJobs.ph:



Your resume (attach as PDF)

Link to your Loom/YouTube intro video

Your specific availability (which hours you can work in CST, Monday–Friday)

Confirm you used exactly 9 Application Points





Application Checklist (Check These Off Before Submitting):



 I used exactly 9 Application Points when applying

 My subject line is: "Sales VA – [My First Name] – 9 AP"

 I included a short intro video less than 2 minutes (Loom, YouTube, or other medium)

 I attached my resume as a PDF

 I confirmed my internet speed and CST availability in my video

 I stated my specific availability hours in my application



Applications that don't follow these instructions exactly will be deleted without review.

This is your first test of attention to detail — the same skill you'll need every day in this role.



Why Work With Us?

? Meaningful mission — You're helping educators take control of their income and build businesses they own

? Growth opportunity — We're scaling fast. Prove yourself and this role can grow with you (more hours, higher pay, expanded responsibilities)

? Direct mentorship — You'll work closely with experienced business builders

? Performance-based rewards — We reward results, not just hours logged

? Supportive, collaborative team — We value proactive communication, ownership, and getting better every week

? Fast-moving environment — We're building in real-time. You'll see the direct impact of your work immediately



We're looking for an eager learner with sales experience, exceptional English skills, and the right cultural fit — someone who sees this as more than just "a VA job."

If that's you, we'd love to hear from you.
